body.title: Evaluator rack device: LLM judge panel for agent output quality
body.result: 3-judge LLM panel rack device shipped: evaluate/rubric_create/rubric_list/eval_history MCP tools, rubrics in adc.palace, 28 unit+integration tests all green
body.description:
A rack device that accepts an agent output + a rubric and returns a scored verdict from a panel of LLM judges. Rubrics are stored as palace nodes (adc.palace) so they accumulate over time. Results feed back into provenance trust tiers (T-provenance-trust-scoring) — an agent that consistently passes evals earns higher trust.

MCP tools exposed: evaluate(output, rubric_id) → {score, verdict, judge_reasoning[]}; rubric_create(name, criteria[]) → rubric_id; rubric_list() → rubric[]; eval_history(agent_id, limit) → eval[].

Judge panel: 3 independent LLM calls with the same rubric, majority verdict. Rubric format: list of named criteria each with pass/fail/score instruction.

**Affected files:** devices/evaluator/ (new), config/profiles/evaluator.yaml
**Design rules:** BaseDevice/BaseShim contract; inference device handles all LLM calls (evaluator does not call LLM directly); rubrics in adc.palace
**Scope boundary:** IN — judge panel, rubric storage, eval history, trust tier integration; OUT — automated rubric generation, eval scheduling (that's nanny's job)
**Completion criteria:** evaluate(output='hello world', rubric_id='R-basic') returns scored verdict with 3 judge reasoning entries; eval history queryable; rubric stored and retrievable from palace
